briefing.headline: HAL nearly dead (-78.7%). SLB is only affordable option. $138 cash available.
briefing.body: Screener ran Jun 9 evening. 7 stocks within 8% of 52w high: UNH (AT high), LLY, CAT, SLB, GE, MPC, ETN. Only 1 tradeable call found within budget: SLB $58C 6/26 at $0.75/sh (3 contracts = $225). Problem: you only have $138 available — $225 is out of reach unless HAL frees up capital. HAL $43C expires Jun 18, currently $0.13/sh (bid=0, ask=0). Needs +6% move to $43 in 9 days — very unlikely. Best path: close HAL at any bid that appears, redeploy into SLB (1 contract = $75 fits budget). UNH is the strongest momentum name (AT 52w high, +9.3% last week) but options are too expensive for this budget.
briefing.action_detail: HAL is effectively dead. Close it as soon as any bid appears (even $0.05). That frees $39 + your $138 = ~$177. Then enter SLB $58C 6/26 at $0.75 — 1 contract ($75) leaves $100 in reserve. Do NOT chase LLY or UNH — premiums exceed budget.

watchlist.name: UnitedHealth Group
watchlist.thesis: AT 52-week high ($413). Best momentum in screener: +9.3% 1w, +9.6% 2w, +8.7% 1m. Exploding breakout. Options too expensive for current $250 budget (ATM calls ~$8-12/sh). Track for when account grows above $1,000.
watchlist.watch_for: Pullback to $395-400 for better entry. Account size needs to reach $1,000+ for options to be viable.
watchlist.day_trade_plan: Not viable at current budget. Monitor for breakout continuation. Revisit when available capital > $500.

watchlist.name: GE Aerospace
watchlist.thesis: 5% from 52w high ($347.97). Consistent uptrend: +4% 1w, +5.1% 2w, +11.2% 1m. Industrial sector leader. Steady, predictable movement — better for options timing than volatile names. Options marginally expensive for budget but worth monitoring.
watchlist.watch_for: Volume pickup above 1.5x average, push above $340, affordable ATM calls under $2.50/sh
watchlist.day_trade_plan: Not viable at current budget. Check options chain weekly — if account reaches $500+ available, enter ATM call 2-3 weeks out.

watchlist.name: SLB (Schlumberger)
watchlist.thesis: 4.55% from 52w high ($58.51). $58C 6/26 available at $0.75/sh — only option that fits budget. WTI crude at $88.89 (above $84 danger threshold). Previously traded SLB successfully (+68.5% in Jun 2). Familiar setup.
watchlist.watch_for: Close HAL first to free capital. Then enter $58C 6/26 at $0.75 — 1 contract ($75) or 2 contracts ($150) depending on HAL recovery.
watchlist.day_trade_plan: Enter $58C 6/26 at $0.75/sh. 1 contract = $75. Target exit: $1.42/sh (+90%). Stop: $0.45/sh (-40%). Exit no later than Jun 23.
watchlist.signal: READY — waiting for HAL capital to free up

watchlist.name: Factorial Energy
watchlist.thesis: Solid-state battery company. Strong macro tailwinds: Iran/Hormuz crisis driving $5 gas, EV adoption urgency, defense/drone demand (IQT-backed). Market growing 43% CAGR. Risk: pre-scale SPAC, thin volume (~10K shares/day). Strategy: observe first hour daily, consider long on morning dip if volume picks up. Do NOT short — macro tailwinds too strong.
watchlist.watch_for: Volume spike above 50K/day, price stabilization above $15, options chain availability
watchlist.day_trade_plan: Buy long on morning dip (first 30-60 min), $100 position, limit sell at +15-20%, same-day exit. Wait for bid/ask spread to tighten before entering.

opportunities.note: Only tradeable option found today. Budget allows 1 contract ($75) now. Close HAL first for 2 contracts ($150). Do not enter more than 2x — leaves reserve.
